title: ranunculus bulbosus subsp. adscendens
intro: Ranunculus bulbosus subsp. adscendens, also known as buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its shiny yellow flowers and potential toxicity. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for wound healing and skin conditions. The health benefits of Ranunculus bulbosus subsp. adscendens include its potential to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, although caution is advised due to its potential toxicity and the need for proper dosing and handling.
